latest.title: Supply Agreement with SeaWith
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 2085X
BSF Enterprise PLC
19 March 2026
1
9 March 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
3D Bio-Tissues Limited (
"
3DBT")
Signs Commercial Supply Agreement with SeaWith
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE: BSFA) (
the
"
Company"),
a leading innovator in tissue-engineered materials, is pleased to announce that its subsidiary, 3D Bio-Tissues Limited (
"
3DBT
"), has entered into a five-year commercial Supply Agreement (the
"
Agreement
") with SeaWith, a pioneering South Korean biotechnology company specializing in cultivated meat.
Strategic Partnership and Value Creation
The Agreement establishes a long-term commercial framework for the supply of 3DBT
'
s proprietary City-Mix®, a premium macromolecular crowder designed to enhance cell culture efficiency. This partnership represents a significant milestone in expanding the market reach of City-Mix® into the global cultivated protein sector.
·
Contract Value
: The total value of the Agreement to 3DBT is estimated at approximately
£300,000
over the contract term.
·
Operational Integration
: 3DBT will provide a regular supply of City-Mix® to support SeaWith
'
s production pipeline for its flagship
Welldone™
cultivated beef product.
·
Cost Efficiency
: City-Mix® is expected to significantly lower production costs for SeaWith by reducing the requirement for expensive growth media by approximately
30%.
·
Growth Trajectory
: The Agreement supports a phased scale-up of supply volumes through 2030, aligning with SeaWith
'
s transition toward large-scale commercial production facilities.
About SeaWith
Founded in 2019 and headquartered in South Korea, SeaWith is a biotechnology innovator that has raised over $8 million in venture funding to date. The company utilizes seaweed-derived technologies to produce sustainable, slaughter-free meat alternatives, focusing on scalability and the reduction of environmental impact. SeaWith is a designated operator under South Korea
'
s Gyeongbuk Cell-Cultured Food Regulatory Free Zone, reflecting the company
'
s leadership in advancing cultivated food innovation within a government-supported regulatory framework.
END

latest.title: LGL Tanning Milestones
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8524W
BSF Enterprise PLC
17 March 2026
17 March 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
Significant Commercial Milestone for Lab-Grown Leather Tanning
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE: BSFA), the tissue-engineering innovator, is pleased to report a major technical and commercial milestone for its wholly-owned subsidiary, Lab-Grown Leather Ltd ("
LGL
"). LGL has successfully tanned scaffold-free, cultivated skin in A4-sized sheets, marking a critical step toward the large-scale commercialisation of sustainable luxury and ultra-luxury materials.
Key Highlights
●
Advanced Tanning Success
: First-of-its-kind tanning achieved in the UK, using a newly developed process that preserves the high-performance qualities of traditional leather.
●
Environmental Impact
: Tanning was completed without the use of heavy metals like chromium by employing only plant-derived, sustainable tanning agents
●
Industry Validation
: Professional tanners have confirmed the material is comparable to traditional leather.
A New Category of Luxury Material
The proprietary process developed by LGL has yielded a totally new form of material. By using tissue engineering to grow scaffold-free skin from the cellular level, the Company has created a bio-equivalent material that maintains the most desirable characteristics of traditional leather - including its natural strength, versatility, distinct smell, and breathability - while developing a unique patina over time.
Crucially, this "Elemental" leather series bypasses the most challenging aspects of traditional leather production. By cultivating only the required dermis and to a specific shape and size, LGL eliminates the need for animal rearing (and its associated land and water use) whilst providing a near zero-waste leather product (minimising the 30% currently lost to trimming). Furthermore, the cultivated skin arrives at the tanning stage without the hair or fats present on raw hides, allowing eliminating the beamhouse processes required for traditional leather. This significantly reduces the cost as well as the chemical and environmental footprint of leather-making.
No Chromium
Importantly, only vegetable tanning components were used, removing the need for using toxic heavy metals. Despite chromium-based leather tanning creating significant environmental concerns, this metal is still used in approximately 90% of global leather production, resulting in hazardous wastewater that contaminates groundwater and aquatic systems.
UK Tanning Success and Collaboration
The tanning of these A4 sheets was successfully completed in the United Kingdom. In a departure from historical industry norms, where similar "behind-closed-doors" tanning was often conducted by in-house luxury fashion teams, LGL worked under a collaborative agreement with seasoned master tanners.
This open-innovation approach allowed for real-time adjustments and expert feedback, resulting in a finish that industry professionals believe is comparable to specific forms of traditional leather. Samples of these A4 sheets are to be shared with a leading global fashion house under a previously established agreement for evaluation and prototype development.
Dr Emily Telford, Principal Scientist at Lab-Grown Leather
Elemental Leather form
The newly tanned material has a pronounced pebble grain highlighting the natural structures, with deckled or "live" edges showing the raw fibers of the cultivated skin. It possesses a matte finish, made possible by not being coated with heavy pigments or plastics,
and consequently
allowing the leather to develop a rich patina over time.
The material has medium-firm temper due in part to the use of vegetable tannins and has a noticeable "spring-back" when bent, thus
being
well suited to
make
wallets, satchels, and high-end cases that need to maintain a crisp, professional silhouette.
In the
touch
, the material has a high "dry-grip" texture, with a dense, fibrous core that feels substantial in the hand, similar to what high-quality hides typically provide.
Being vegetable-tanned, it provides the cultivated leather with strong eco-friendly credentials whilst retaining extreme durability.
Forthcoming T-Rex Leather™ Showcase and Unveiling
In addition to the progress with increased sample sizes and scalable process, the Company is pleased to confirm the upcoming T-Rex Leather™ showcase and unveiling taking place in Amsterdam on April 2nd.
In partnership with VML and The Orgainoid Company, LGL will reveal the world's first consumer ultra-luxury item crafted from T-Rex LeatherTM. This material, engineered using fossilized
Tyrannosaurus Rex
collagen as a genetic blueprint, will demonstrate the power of LGL's platform to create "extinct luxury" materials that are ethically produced and entirely unique to the market.
Che Connon, CEO of BSF Enterprise, commented:
"The successful tanning of A4-sized sheets in the UK is the 'proof of concept' the industry has been waiting for. We have shown that we can preserve the soul of leather-the smell, the feel, the durability-while removing the ethical and environmental baggage. ElementalTM is not a compromise but a technological upgrade. Working collaboratively with tanners has accelerated our progress significantly, and we look forward to revealing the fruits of this labour in Amsterdam and with our fashion house partners."
END

latest.title: Further Update on Fundraise & Reorganisation
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4662W
BSF Enterprise PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
("BSF" or the "Company")
Further Update on Proposed £15m Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation
Further to the Company's announcement on 27 February 2026 the Company provides an update on the previously announced Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation. Capitalised terms used in this announcement have the same meanings as in the earlier announcement unless the context requires otherwise.
The Equity Fundraise for £15m and proposed Capital Reorganisation has been mutually terminated between the parties and each party will seek to terminate all relevant agreements that formed part of the transaction.
However the CLN of £300K will continue
on the same terms but be extend for 12 months and then repaid in cash or the CLN noteholders have a right to convert over that period at next BSF capital raise price.
Further to the above, the Company confirms that it will seek alternative fundraising options and it has entered discussions with other parties to raise funds both at Group level and subsidiary level for Lab-grown Leather Ltd and Kerato Ltd.
This process remains ongoing and the Company is seeking to close a new favourable fundraising option in the coming weeks. The Company will provide further updates as appropriate.
END

latest.title: First Tannable A4-Sized Skin Achieved
latest.content:
Today 10:47
RNS Number : 0672W
BSF Enterprise PLC
10 March 2026
10 March 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
First Tannable A4-Sized Skin Achieved
Scalable Process
Unlocked for Elemental™
; T-Rex Leather™ Showcase Confirmed for 2 April 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE:BSFA), the tissue-engineering innovator, is pleased to report a significant commercial milestone for its wholly-owned subsidiary Lab-Grown Leather Limited ("
LGL
"): the successful production of a fully tanned, scaffold-free skin reaching A4 sheet size and approaching 2mm thickness - achieved in a matter of weeks using LGL's proprietary Advanced Tissue Engineering Platform ("
ATEP™
").
Key Milestones Achieved
• A4-size tannable skin produced at industrially relevant thickness (~2mm)
• Scalable process developed over the last six months using ATEP™
• Scaffold-free architecture delivering bio-equivalent durability, breathability and patina
• Zero-waste geometry: skin cultivated to specification, eliminating traditional hide scrap rates
• T-Rex Leather™ website and social channels live; showcase event confirmed for 2 April 2026
Elemental™: A New Standard for High-Performance Luxury Materials
The Elemental™ product series represents the commercial embodiment of LGL's ATEP™. Over the past six months, the LGL team has engineered a repeatable, scalable process that takes animal cells and cultivates them into a tannable skin product at dimensions that are meaningful to luxury goods manufacturers. This first full tanning of the material - a critical processing step required before leather can be used in finished goods - confirms that Elemental™ is advancing through the technical readiness pathway towards commercial supply.
Unlike many competing bio-based or synthetic alternative materials, LGL's scaffold-free approach means the cells self-organise to produce a material that is bio-equivalent to conventional hide: retaining the structural integrity, tactile properties, and characteristic patina that luxury brands demand. The process can be tuned to produce leathers that are thinner, stronger, or with unique surface characteristics that would be impossible or unethical to source conventionally. Thus LGL is able to uniquely position ElementalTM as a high-end, low-intervention material, setting the standard for a new generation of leather and cultivating a new era for luxury.
T-Rex Leather™ - Where Technology Meets Rarity
One of the most distinctive and commercially compelling applications of LGL's platform is its ability to engineer material inspired by the cellular biology of rare and extinct species. T-Rex Leather™ leverages this capability to offer the luxury and fashion industries access to materials that are, by definition, unavailable by any other means - creating a genuine scarcity narrative that is entirely authentic and ethically sound.
This positions LGL in a unique category: not simply a "more sustainable" version of existing leather, but a genuinely new class of luxury material with intrinsic rarity, provenance, and a powerful story. The T-Rex Leather™ brand has now launched its website and social media presence, and a showcase event has been confirmed for 2 April 2026, which the Company expects to generate significant press and industry engagement.
See links:
https://www.instagram.com/t_rex_leather/
https://lab-grown-leather.com/t-rex-leather/
Elemental™ skin sample - A4 size, approaching 2mm thickness
Commercial Outlook
The achievement of a tannable skin at A4 scale and industrially relevant thickness is a key gate in LGL's commercialisation roadmap. The Company is now in a position to extend its engagement with luxury goods manufacturers, material distributors, and brand partners on the basis of a tangible, scalable and demonstrable product. LGL's IP-protected ATEP™ platform provides a defensible competitive moat across both the conventional luxury leather market and the emerging exotic/extinct species segment.
The Company will continue to update shareholders on progress, including the outcome of the 2 April 2026 T-Rex Leather™ showcase event.
END

company_30s.what_they_do: BSFA Ltd is a niche alternative‑materials firm that turns animal‑free processes into high‑quality synthetic leather.  Their flagship product is a thin, durable skin that can be printed or woven, and they’re building a supply chain that could replace conventional tanning.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global leather market is under pressure from sustainability concerns.  BSFA’s technology could capture a share of that shift by offering a cruelty‑free, lower‑carbon alternative that still meets the tactile and visual standards of luxury brands.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early‑stage development phase.  It has been raising capital, re‑organising its structure, and securing supply agreements, but it has not yet shown any revenue.  Its share price sits at a very low level, reflecting the uncertainty around product launch and commercialisation.

rns_narrative: The latest filings outline a two‑phase plan: first, a fund‑raise and organisational shift to bring in new expertise; second, a series of supply agreements that will allow the company to scale its production once the prototype is proven.  The company is also reporting progress on key technical milestones—having produced its first A‑4‑sized test sheet of the synthetic skin.
These events cluster around a single theme: moving from lab‑scale proof‑of‑concept to a viable commercial offering that can attract major fashion or automotive clients.
The narrative suggests that the company is stabilising its operations and positioning itself for a market launch, but the timeline remains vague and the success of the technology is still unproven.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: quiet anticipation – people are waiting for a product reveal but no one is actively buying.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are excited about the upcoming showcase of the ‘T‑rex leather’ and the potential for a breakout if the product meets hype.  They see this as a rare, high‑reward play.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point out that the company has zero revenue, a fragile supply chain, and no proven market demand.  They worry the product may never reach commercial scale, leaving the shares stranded.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is moving from speculative curiosity to a cautious wait‑and‑see stance.  The volume of posts has not increased, indicating that the community is still on the sidelines.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the company’s name is flat, meaning there is no current search interest.  This gives a contrarian trader a clean slate: any news could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal product launch or demo at a major industry event (e.g., the upcoming fashion tech expo) could spark a price spike.
A positive funding announcement that secures the capital needed to move from prototype to production would validate the business model.
Securing a first‑time order from a well‑known brand would prove market demand and create a credibility boost.
A regulatory approval or safety certification for the synthetic skin would remove a key barrier to entry.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume (above 3‑month average) coupled with a price break above the last 20‑day moving average.  Also monitor the next RNS for a clear commitment to production timelines or a partnership announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: At present the stock shows no evidence of a completed crash—there is no clear bottom to rebound from.  The APEX score is low, so the market has not yet tightened on the security.  The best case scenario would be a catalyst that forces a rapid mean reversion, but that requires a clear event to break the current stagnation.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the lack of a proven crash, coupled with the high uncertainty around product viability, makes a bullish case too speculative at this point.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company delivers a successful product demo and signs a first order, the stock could jump 200–300% from its current base, reflecting the high upside typical of a deep‑bottom play.  The asymmetry comes from a small downside (the share could fall to zero) versus a large upside if the market embraces the new material.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Failure to secure a commercial partner or to deliver the product on time could leave the shares with no path to revenue, making the downside essentially a total loss of the invested capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100% of the investment if the company fails to commercialise.  Potential gain: up to 3× or more if the product launches successfully and the market adopts it.  The reward is roughly triple the risk, but the probability of the upside is low without a clear catalyst.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a clear catalyst (product demo or partnership) that triggers a volume surge.  Use a small position (5–10% of the portfolio) and set a stop just below the recent swing low to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Monitor the next RNS for a definitive production plan or a major partnership.  Watch for a 20% jump in volume paired with a 5% price increase as a signal to consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure any commercial agreement within 6–9 months of the last RNS, consider cutting the position to avoid a prolonged dead‑weight loss.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BSFA Ltd sits at a crossroads: a potentially game‑changing synthetic leather, but no proven market traction yet.  Until a clear product launch or partnership materialises, the risk of a total wipe‑out outweighs the upside, making a cautious, watch‑and‑wait stance the safest approach.
